examples: We have to live with hexavalent chromium and manage it at the source.
California's proposed health goal is designed to limit possible cancer risk to just one in a million for those exposed to hexavalent chromium.
CARCINOGENIC chromium: Turning hides into leather in much of the world requires chromium, which in its hexavalent chemical form is a potent carcinogen.
Other solutions are more complex, but still cheap, such as injecting a sugary mixture into a water supply contaminated by hexavalent chromium to make it less toxic and less mobile underground.
C. was among localities with contaminated water -- hexavalent chromium.
